name: OWASP API Security Top 10
description: The most critical API security risks, mapped to Azure mitigations. Summaries are paraphrased.

controls.title: Broken Object Level Authorization
controls.summary: APIs expose object IDs without verifying the caller may access that specific object.
controls.remediation: Check ownership/authorization on every object access server-side; use unpredictable IDs as defense in depth; test BOLA in CI.

controls.title: Broken Authentication
controls.summary: Flawed token handling or weak flows let attackers assume other identities.
controls.remediation: Validate Entra ID JWTs (issuer, audience, signature, expiry) on every request; short token lifetimes; APIM validate-jwt policy.

controls.title: Broken Object Property Level Authorization
controls.summary: APIs expose or accept object properties the caller should not read or write (excessive data exposure / mass assignment).
controls.remediation: Use explicit response DTOs and input schemas (Pydantic), never bind requests directly to data models.

controls.title: Unrestricted Resource Consumption
controls.summary: Missing rate limits and quotas enable DoS and cost abuse.
controls.remediation: APIM rate-limit and quota policies, request size limits, pagination caps, and autoscale with budget alerts.

controls.title: Broken Function Level Authorization
controls.summary: Administrative or privileged endpoints lack role checks.
controls.remediation: Enforce role checks (app roles) per endpoint with deny-by-default routing; separate admin API surface.

controls.title: Unrestricted Access to Sensitive Business Flows
controls.summary: Legitimate flows (purchasing, posting) are abused at scale without anti-automation controls.
controls.remediation: Bot protection at the edge, per-account velocity limits, and business-flow anomaly monitoring.

controls.title: Server-Side Request Forgery
controls.summary: APIs fetching user-supplied URLs can be steered at internal targets.
controls.remediation: Allow-list outbound hosts, block metadata endpoints, and isolate fetching components in restricted egress subnets.

controls.title: Security Misconfiguration
controls.summary: Missing hardening across the API stack: verbose errors, permissive CORS, absent headers.
controls.remediation: Strict CORS, security headers, sanitized errors, disable unused HTTP verbs, config scanning in CI.

controls.title: Improper Inventory Management
controls.summary: Forgotten API versions and undocumented endpoints stay exposed and unpatched.
controls.remediation: Catalog all APIs in APIM, retire old versions with sunset policies, and generate OpenAPI docs from code.

controls.title: Unsafe Consumption of APIs
controls.summary: Downstream/third-party API responses are trusted blindly, propagating injection or bad data.
controls.remediation: Validate and sanitize third-party responses against schemas, apply timeouts/circuit breakers, and pin TLS.
